type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
spec: Add optional keyword-only parameter transform_callback to exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, transform_callback=None). Signature: transform_callback: Callable[[int], Image.Transpose | Sequence[Image.Transpose] | None] | None = None. Behavior: after computing orientation = image_exif.get(ExifTags.Base.Orientation, 1), if transform_callback is not None, call method = transform_callback(orientation) INSTEAD of the built-in orientation->method dict lookup (the callback fully overrides the default mapping - default dict is only used when transform_callback is None). The callback's return value may be: (a) a single Image.Transpose enum value - apply exactly like the existing single-method code path (image.transpose(method) / image.im.transpose(method)), or (b) a sequence (list/tuple) of Image.Transpose values - apply them in order, each via successive .transpose() calls (for in_place, apply each transpose in sequence, reassigning image.im and image._size after each; for not-in_place, start from image and chain transpose() calls in order to build transposed_image), or (c) None - meaning no transformation should be applied (skip the transpose branch entirely, equivalent to method is None in the existing code), even if the raw orientation tag lookup would otherwise have produced a method. After the transform(s) are applied (or skipped), the existing behavior must be unchanged: strip the Orientation exif tag and update exif_image.info exactly as today. Edge cases: transform_callback receives the raw integer orientation value (whatever getexif() returned, including 1 or any value not in the standard dict, e.g. could be 0 or 9+, since callback may implement custom handling for values the default mapping doesn't cover) - do not restrict/validate the orientation value before passing it to the callback. If transform_callback is None (default), behavior must be 100% identical to current implementation. Update the docstring to document the new transform_callback parameter, its signature/purpose, and that it overrides the default orientation mapping.
note: transform_callback overrides method lookup in exif_transpose; supports single Transpose, sequence of Transpose, or None

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Add strip_metadata param to exif_transpose(). Signature: def exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False) -- add strip_metadata as a keyword-only bool param (default False) to all three overloads/impl (the two @overload stubs and the real def), positioned after in_place in the signature. Docstring: add ':param strip_metadata: Boolean. Keyword-only argument. If True, all metadata (exif, icc_profile, xmp, and other info dict metadata keys) is removed from the returned/modified image after the transpose+orientation-removal logic runs. Default False preserves current behavior (only orientation tag removed).' Behavior: after the existing orientation-transpose logic completes (right before the final 'if not in_place: return transposed_image' / 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' / 'return None' block), if strip_metadata is True, strip all metadata from exif_image (in_place case) or transposed_image/returned image (not in_place case) -- including the case where orientation==1 and no transpose happened (the 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' branch, and the in_place-no-transpose case where method is None). Concretely: clear exif_image.info dict entirely (info = {}), and clear the exif data (exif_image.getexif().clear() or equivalent so exif is empty), covering exif, icc_profile, dpi, xmp, comment, etc. Must work correctly in_place=True/False and transpose/no-transpose combinations (4 total branches). Backward compatible: default False means zero behavior change.
note: exif_transpose

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: ack: strip_metadata bool kw-only param, default False, strips all info/exif metadata after transpose logic in all in_place/not-in_place x transpose/no-transpose branches
note: Acknowledged - I will own both files and implement both transform_callback and strip_metadata per your SPEC. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Please add a test for the new strip_metadata param on exif_transpose. Suggested test: open Tests/images/exif-72dpi-int.tiff (or any test image with exif orientation + other metadata such as dpi/icc) via Image.open, call ImageOps.exif_transpose(im, strip_metadata=True), then assert the returned image has no exif orientation tag AND that other metadata is gone too (e.g. transposed.getexif() is empty/falsy, and transposed.info does not contain keys like 'exif', 'icc_profile', 'dpi' that existed on the original if applicable). Also add a check that strip_metadata=False (default) preserves existing metadata-stripping-only-orientation behavior (no regression) - can reuse an existing test image already used in test_exif_transpose tests in that file. Also test in_place=True combined with strip_metadata=True if convenient. Function name suggestion: test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata or similar, placed near existing exif_transpose tests.
note: test coverage for strip_metadata param

Existing test_imageops.py suite (34 tests) still passes. Ready for your test additions in Tests/test_imageops.py.
